lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here? I see a lot of farming patches with nothing growing in them.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: No, one has just had them installed. One has had the most marvellous idea to bring renewed happiness to one's own dearest husband.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One? I'm not sure I understand you-

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh dear - the common classes, oh how they fill one with intolerable levels of exasperation, I swear to be most true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ri-ight ... if you say so, my lady. I'll be off then, if you don't mind-

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes, be off with you, before I call the guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem, sorry to have bothered you.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Peasant - WAIT!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you speaking to me?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Who else would I be speaking to, you funny little fool? Of course you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well for a moment there I thought there was a dog around here somewhere...

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh yes, sarcasm - how amusing. No, no, peasant - I have a question to ask of you, and you are required to answer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, seeing as you put it so nicely...

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Do you know of anyone who might have some degree of horticultural experience?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Well, hurry off then. You are of no use to me.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh, marvellous! Peasant, I hereby order you to assist me with a little project I have planned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll have to ask me nicely first.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh, what I have to deal with these days - to think this is what being a Queen has become. Very well then: would you ... good sir help me with a little project of mine?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh dear, what a pathetic creature you are. One would give you a list, but you do not appear to have room enough to carry it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would be happy to help someone who is so in touch with the people.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Ah, more sarcasm - wonderful. Well, it is nice to have a master of the fine art of gardening to assist me; in the absence of any such person, I gladly accept your service.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if that's a compliment or not. Anyway, what would you like me to do, and what do I get out of it?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: To answer your first question, you have noticed the new farming patches that we have had installed around us. We wish to grow many varied and exotic plants in them, to bring happiness back to my husband.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Roald?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: The very same, and that's 'His Most Royal Majesty' to you. Now we come to the plants that one wishes you to grow in these patches. I would tell you all at once, but I fear that your small mind would not cope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'm sure. So tell me Queeny, how do I know which plants I am to get for you?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: I have given you a list of the items that I need in each patch. One is sure that you will know how to use the list with each patch to find out what needs to go in each one. There is one more thing, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One listens with bated breath...

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Obtaining the seeds may prove difficult if you go about it by hacking and slashing everything that moves. No, to gather these items you will need to employ a finer tactic; the art of persuasion.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: You must go at once and converse with the wise old man who is said to dwell in Draynor Village; he may guide you in mastering this delicate and sophisticated ability.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather jump off the docks at Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Well don't allow one to delay you - it is probably the most constructive thing you could do with your life, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have another list?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Take better care with this one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I supposed to move statues all the way here?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: You use the dormant object that lies between your ears, that's how. A statue may be far too heavy to carry, but simplicity itself to push.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Push?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes peasant, push. One would give you something to help with this task, but you do not appear to have room enough to carry it.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes peasant, push. Here - take this trolley. You can use it to push the statues here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What I am supposed to be getting again?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Haven't you got that list I gave you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err ... no ... I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh dear, what a pathetic creature you are. One would give you another list, but you do not appear to have room enough to carry it.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh dear, what a pathetic creature you are. Here, have another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will these patches need much in the way of watering?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Well, you are supposed to be the gardener here, but I would say not. One had the soil imported from an extremely fertile area at great cost - watering should not be necessary one would have thought.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Very good peasant, thank you for showing one that, but one has given you a job to do. Do it.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: I want you to place a statue of Saradomin there.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: I want you to place a statue of a Misthalin king there.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: I want you to grow yellow orchids there.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: I want you to grow pink orchids there.

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man
Talking to the Wise Old Man again after being told to bring him the Ring of Charos

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I re-obtain the Ring of Charos?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have returned to you with the Ring of Charos. Now will you reveal its 'hidden power beyond belief'?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I don't see it on you - do you have it in your bag?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, no - I've stored it somewhere.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well go and get it then, and I will tell you its secrets.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The Ring of Charos? What's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you told me to-

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Just joking, [player name] - good good, I will now tell you what this ring is capable of.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Charos rose to power under the rule of Lord Drakan of Morytania, his only living servant. The Vampyre Lord suffered the existence of Charos because of the talents that this sophisticated diplomat had to offer.

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man again after attempting the diplomacy test

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I retake the diplomacy test?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Do you have the ring with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm afraid that I lost it at some point.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Do you have the ring with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it stored in the bank. Should I go and get it then?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, yes get it and return back to me.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, if you think it will do any good ... here goes then-

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man again after he activates the Ring of Charos

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Queen Ellamaria has sent me to seek your guidance.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ellamaria? Well, if you're in her service my guidance is this: do the fastest run that you can off the shortest pier you can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know Queen Ellamaria?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Oh yes, we go way back, long before she married that fool the King of Varrock. She wasn't always royal you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: No, in her youth she was a barmaid at the Blue Moon inn. All that fancy talk, that's just for show. No, the Ellamaria I knew was as common as muck, until she lucked onto good King Roald.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll certainly look at her in a different light when I return to Varrock.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So what sort of guidance has Little-Miss-Hoity-Toity sent you to seek?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am supposed to gain some knowledge of diplomacy and persuasion from you.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: That is an area that - luckily for you, young [player name] - I have studied. Have you heard of the diplomat known as Charos?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Charos? I must say the name rings a bell...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Charos...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I remember! Once, during my travels to Morytania, I came upon an item of magical jewellery called the Ring of Charos.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You have found the Ring of Charos?!? Tell me - do you still have it in your possession?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm wearing it at the moment. Is it important, then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it in my bag. Is it important, then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it stored somewhere. Is it important, then?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Important?!? This ring possesses hidden power beyond belief! Go and get it, and I will tell you its secrets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm afraid that I lost it at some point.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Lost the Ring of Charos?!? That is indeed a disastrous turn of events. This ring that you have so casually thrown away possesses hidden power beyond belief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I re-obtain the Ring of Charos?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If everything I have heard about Charos's ring is true, it will not stay lost forever. Where did you originally find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I ... um ... 'borrowed' it from a rather shady character called Fenkenstrain.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: In order to find the ring it may be wise to return to Fenkenstrain - he may have found a way to regain it. If you manage to reobtain the ring, return to me and I will reveal its secrets to you.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Charos rose to power under the rule of Lord Drakan of Morytania, his only living servant. The Vampyre Lord suffered the existence of Charos because of the talents that this sophisticated diplomat had to offer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The art of persuasion?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Indeed. Charos had many uses - spy, assassin, negotiator - because he could convince anyone to do his wishes. These talents were in some part Charos's own, but greatly enhanced by the ring that he fashioned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Ring of Charos.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Exactly. One who wears the ring must possess some innate ability of their own, which the ring enhances so that the wearer can convince all but the most strong- minded to do their bidding.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to Charos? And why did Fenkenstrain have the ring?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Charos had a castle built on the northern coast of Morytania, where he dwelt for many years in service to Drakan. None really know what became of him, but some say that he became too certain of his abilities,

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: and turned them on his master. This would have been his ultimate downfall, for the ring's power would have had little or no effect upon Drakan.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Fenkenstrain probably found the ring many years later, buried somewhere within the depths of Charos's castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what you are telling me is that nobody really knows what happened to Charos? That he could still be alive? And if he is still alive...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: ...he would naturally come looking for the ring that is his by right. But I don't believe that you should worry about that - it has been many years since his disappearance, and the likelyhood is that he is dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still - it doesn't inspire me with much confidence.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I could activate the power within this ring for you, but for me to do that I must know that you will use it wisely. To do that I must ask you a few questions, to see how good a diplomat you can be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, go ahead...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I've answered your questions - what's the verdict?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Mmmm, it was close but..

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: ... but I'm afraid that you don't seem to have the aptitude. To you, the Ring of Charos is but a worthless trinket.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't believe that - can I have another go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, if you think it will do any good ... here goes then-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well, that's that I suppose.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now look, don't start making me feel sorry for you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm just a dull-headed moron, you're right about that.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I didn't say that - look, if you want another try, come back and speak to me again.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: ... Congratulations! You have clearly got what it takes to use the Ring of Charos. Wait a moment, [player name]...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The Ring of Charos has now been opened to you, [player name]. You may use its power to charm the weak-minded, but it will only work whilst you are wearing it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you wanted to give me a thousand coins...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Ha ha! I thought you might try that!! But remember, [player name] - the power of the Ring of Charos will not work with everybody, especially not a wise old bird like me!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The Ring of Charos has now been opened to you, [player name]. You may use its power to charm the weak-minded, but it will only work whilst you are wearing it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help, wise old man.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Funny, I thought you would take the chance to test the power of the ring on me! No matter - leave me be now, I have important surveillance duties to carry out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have those marigolds for you.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Hang on, I didn't see you growing any marigolds! I want them grown here, in this patch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But they'll be no different to any other marigolds!

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Do you want those seeds or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're going to be like that, it'll take more than flowers to make Lyra want to date you.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Ah, fantastic - Lyra will surely be mine now! Here you go mate, here's those delphinium seeds you were after.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Yes, here you go - I hope they grow for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did I agree to do for delphinium seeds?

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: I need some marigolds so that I can woo Lyra. Grow them from seed to flower in this flower patch here, pick them and give them to me, and I'll give you some delphinium seeds.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: I can't believe you're asking me to do that! I asked you to grow some marigolds for me, not for me to look after them for you! No, I won't look after them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have some more delphinium seeds?

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: I would give you some, but you don't appear to have room to carry them.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Yes, here you go - I hope they grow for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any delphinium seeds to spare?

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: I do have delphinium seeds, yes - but to spare, I'm not so sure. You don't look like that much of a gardener to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have you know I'm a pretty good farmer, actually!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is why I have come to an expert for advice.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Ah, now that I like the sound of. So people are saying I'm an expert, then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I just said that to get you to give me some seeds.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Really?!? Well, give me any more of that kind of talk and I'll give you something to remember me by, and I'll tell you now that it ain't seeds!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, carry on - milk it for all it's worth.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: What?!? Any more of that talk and it'll be curtains for you, mate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just AN expert, Elstan - they say you are THE expert.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: I can't say that I'm surprised, really - I do seem to have the golden touch with the old gardening lark. Only just the other day I managed to grow a - I'm not boring you, am I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please stop right now.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: As a matter of fact I will! I'm not wasting my time on the likes of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no, I love listening to gardening stories...

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: You do! That's the first person I've ever met has said that! Well, don't you worry mate, I've got millions of 'em!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somebody kill me now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Millions? Ah, just what I wanted to hear...

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Ah, but there's plenty of time for stories. You wanted delphinium seeds, did you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er .. what ... er ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, delphinium seeds!

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: As I'm in an especially good mood I think I will let you have some. But you have to do something for me first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what's that?

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: I need some marigolds to give to Lyra, one of my fellow gardeners over in Morytania. She's a bit mysterious, and has some - well - sinister ways to be sure, but she's just my type.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, a bunch of marigolds then?

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Yes, and while I'm at it I can see how good a gardener you are. Grow those marigolds in this flower patch here from seed to flower, pick them and give them to me, and I'll give you some delphinium seeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have those marigolds for you.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Ah, fantastic - Lyra will surely be mine now! Here you go mate, here's those delphinium seeds you were after. Yes, here you go – I hope they grow for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll grow you some marigolds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have time to do that.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Have it your own way, mate. No seeds for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure you don't just want to give me the seeds now?

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Strange - there's this funny buzzing in my head that says that I should - but I don't think I will. I need those marigolds for Lyra.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you ask me to do?

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: I need you to grow some onions in one of these vegetable patches, from seed until they are fully grown - then I will give you some orchid seeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I've grown those onions like you asked.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Yes, you have demonstrated that you have some moderate skills as a farmer, but I fear that you would not have the space to carry them.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Yes, you have demonstrated that you have some moderate skills as a farmer. Take these seeds, and do with them what you will.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: I can't believe you're asking me to do that! I asked you to grow some onions for me, not for me to look after them for you! No, I won't look after them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have some more orchid seeds?

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: I would give you some orchid seeds, but I fear that you would not have the space to carry them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any orchid seeds to spare?

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Please, do not bother me - I have problems to attend to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh sorry, I didn't mean to take up your valuable time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you tell me your problems, I may be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: As you ask so nicely I will relate my problems to you, but I very much doubt that you may help me at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Try me.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: You must think it strange that a girl such as myself would choose to ply her trade in a place such as this, this darkest of lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not really - you fit in here a treat.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Then begone simple fool and halt your churlish tongue. There are things about me that would leave you with nightmares for the rest of your life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I was wondering about that.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: My mother, Alice, tends to our farming shop in yonder farmhouse, while I tend to this small allotment. Passing trade has quite literally died off in recent years, and with it our income.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can only say one thing to you - move.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: And leave behind all that we possess?!? Never!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Times must be very hard for you.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: It is but the smallest of our worries. We get by, we survive, on the miserable harvests that I grow here. The price of our survival is what pains me, as it is a price I have paid with the souls of others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, it all makes sense now - you're a psychopath.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: If your time is so pressing then leave me be and go about your business!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what is the nature of this fee that you pay?

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Mother and I must pay a tithe of blood to the vampyres. Father would have protected us, but he is no longer with us, although his presence still walks among us in the fields. We pay with the blood of others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, can I withdraw my offer of help now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If not yours, then whose blood are you offering?

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Sometimes I travel to market fairs in distant lands, and I use my feminine arts to lure young farmers back to this land; then the vampyres come for them and I see them no more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something tells me that you actually enjoy it, really.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: I would not talk so if I were you - I know a secret call that will bring the vampyres here right now, and then you may live only long enough to feel regret for your impertinence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you deal with so much guilt?

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Please - ask me no more. You said that you were looking for some orchid seeds? I do have some as a matter of fact, and you may as well have them because they do not grow well in this grim land.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, can I have them then...

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: You must pass a test first. These are no ordinary flowers, and no ordinary farmer can grow them. You must grow onions here in one of these patches, and when they are fully grown I will give you the seeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a deal – I'll grow a patch of onions for you.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Speak to me when they are fully grown. You need not pick them for me, I will be able to assess your skills by simply watching you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think not – I've loitered too long in this land.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Yes, I agree - far too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh for Saradomin's sake, just give me the seeds!

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: This feeling in my head - it is so strange - and it is a feeling I have felt before, although I cannot explain it. If you are trying to use some unnatural power over me, you have failed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you ask me to do?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: You said that you would grow me some cabbages in one of these allotments. When they're ready for harvest come and talk to me, and I'll give you those snowdrop seeds that you're after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I've grown those cabbages like you asked.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Yes, I saw - what fine farming prowess! Nobody has ever done anything like that for me before; here you go, these are the seeds you were after I believe.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: I would give you some, but you don't appear to have room to carry them.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Yes, here you go - I hope they grow for you.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: I can't believe you're asking me to do that! I asked you to grow some cabbages for me, not for me to look after them for you! No, I won't look after them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have some more snowdrop seeds?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Yes, here you go - I hope they grow for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any snowdrop seeds to spare?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: No I don't, as it happens. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I was told that you did!

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Well, you were told wrong then!

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Goodbye!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem to be a little irritable, my friend.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Oh, you think so?!? And what concern is it of yours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not concerned, I just want you to give me some snowdrop seeds.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Is that right?!? Well, I do actually have some snowdrop seeds, but you would be the last person I would give them to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't like to see a fellow human being so upset.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: I must say, you don't often meet people who show concern the way you have done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't take it anymore - just give me the seeds.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: And to think I listened to you! I was right after all - there is no kindness between human beings anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what ails you, my friend?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: All these people, asking me to look after their crops! And no-one ever asking me how I feel, or if there's something they can do for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh stop whining, it's not like you do it for free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Oh, that's very kind of you! And there I was, thinking of giving it all up and heading off across the seas to find my fortune!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't let me stop you, there are plenty of ships missing cabin boys.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: If you carry on speaking to me like that I might just put this spade to good use, and I'm not talking about digging!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: I'd like just for once for somebody to grow something for me. Just something simple; some vegetables would be nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about some potatoes?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Nah, there's a whole field of them over to the west. I don't have any particular use for onions, so how about cabbages, then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cabbages? Sounds good to me. Where do you want me to grow them?

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Oh, in one of these allotments here. Plant them from seed, and when they're ready for harvesting let me know, I'll give you your seeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a deal - I'll let you know when your cabbages are ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not wasting my time growing cabbages.

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: And I'm not wasting my seeds on an upstart like you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aarrrrrghhhh! I hate cabbages!!!! Give me the seeds!!!!!

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Give ... you ... the ...

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: ... yes I should ...

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: ... no I won't ...

lines.speaker: Kragen
lines.text: Not after speaking to me like that you upstart!!! Is it a deal or not?!?

sections.title: Talking to Bernald after trying to cure the vines

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Plant cure not doing the trick, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand - it's worked with everything else.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: That grapevine disease must be much stronger than the usual. You'll need something a bit more potent to cut out that infection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know of any stronger cures?

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: No, I don't - but I know of a man who might. Not far from here there's a druid gardener who goes by the name of Alain. He's an expert on things like this, so they tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why didn't you just go and ask him yourself?

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: These vines are very precious indeed. I could not hazard the risk that someone would steal my grapes while I was away - they may be diseased, but they still contain Burthorpe Vine seeds.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Do you know anything about grapevine diseases?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might - what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: You see those grapevines over there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. They don't look too happy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: They've come down with some strange disease that I've never encountered before. I've tried everything with them, but nothing seems to work. If it carries on like this I will lose all this year's crops.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could probably help you with that, but I'd need something in return.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Anything, you just need to ask!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'd like a supply of Burthorpe Vine seeds.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you said anything!

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: I'm afraid that is the one thing I cannot give you. Burthorpe Vine has been bred over many years and many past generations of my family. I could never give away something so special.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it is the only way that these vines will be cured.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Hmm, you may be right. If I lose these crops what use are more seeds to me, when I have gone out of business? Very well then, this is the deal - cure my vines and I will give you some Burthorpe Vine seeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, it seems like a lot of hard work for nothing.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: I wouldn't call my livelihood 'nothing' if I were you. Very well, I shall find someone else to assist me.

sections.title: Talking to Bernald with the stronger plant cure

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Have you spoken to Alain yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. He has given me some knowledge so that I can make a more potent plant cure.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Let's hope that it does the job, then.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: My vines are cured! You have worked a wonder here, [player name]! Here, you can have those seeds like I promised.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: I would give you some, but you don't appear to have room to carry them.

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Yes, here you go - I hope they grow for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any more Burthorpe Vine seeds?

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: What did you do with the ones I gave you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm...

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Yes, here you go - I hope they grow for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are your grapes coming along?

lines.speaker: Bernald
lines.text: Marvellously, thanks to your help, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to ask you about strong plant cures.

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: Well, you have come to the right person, then - I researched exotic plant diseases as part of my druidic training. What is the affliction that you need to cure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bernald up in Burthorpe has got some seriously diseased grapevines. I tried plant cure on them, but it didn't seem to have any positive effect.

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: Ah, so is this the famous Burthorpe Vine?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The very one.

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: I'm afraid that my researches did not extend to Burthorpe Vine. Bernald and his ancestors have always closely guarded their heritage, that vine. In fact, I'm surprised that he has involved you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I did have some ... assistance there.

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: I see. Well, if it involves some unnatural power I would rather not know. I prefer the use of power that can be brought forth from the plants around us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure there's nothing you can suggest?

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: There is one thing you could try. One of the most potent cures I have discovered involves using the natural power held within a very special mineral, but it is a very closely guarded secret.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mean the rune essence?

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: You know of that?!? Then I can do no further harm here. You can make a potent plant cure by mixing some crumbled rune essence into a normal plant cure. This may possibly cure Bernald's vines.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you Alain, I will try it out immediately!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're hiding something from me.

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: This is the power you are abusing!! I feel it now - it emanates from the ring you wear, a festering fountain of corruption that appalls my very soul!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry. I forgot that it only works on the weak- minded.

lines.speaker: Alain
lines.text: You, [player name], are the one who must be weak- minded, if you consider using such a dreadful weapon. Beware - it may one day be your downfall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the cutting from the White Tree.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: I do not believe what I am hearing, after everything you said to me before!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yes I know ... sorry ...

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Maybe - just maybe - there might be another shoot somewhere on the Tree that you can take as a cutting. Go and havea look, before she dies without offspring!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here is the shoot that Dantaera spoke of. Everything she told me is true – this tree will die once I cut this shoot. Well, here goes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have already cut the shoot from the White Tree. I should quickly grow a new tree in Queen Ellamaria's garden.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, luckily there is another shoot here that I can cut. Better not lose this one...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how I could grow a White Tree?

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: There are no more White Trees left in the world - they have all been chopped down, and their beauty has forever left this world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then my quest is over - I cannot grow a White Tree.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Even if there were a White Tree somewhere I would not tell you, for as soon as it would grow then it would be slaughtered in its prime. Man's hunger for fire is greater than his admiration of beauty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that there is something that you are not telling me.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Am I so transparent? You have seen through me; there is something I would rather not tell you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need to chill out and stop taking yourself so seriously.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Leave me in peace then, if you are so unable to understand my grief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A secret is a dreadful burden to have to keep to yourself.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: It is not hard to keep this secret - to give it away would cause me great sorrow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't see how. Tell me your secret, and I will keep it as well as you have done.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Very well - I feel deep trust in you for some reason. It is the White Tree, last survivor of a race of Trees that have disappeared from our landscape. Once they covered the land; now there is only one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to them?

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Man desired the wood of the White Tree, because of its wood of pure white. Need I tell you more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can fill in the rest. So where is the remaining White Tree?

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: She stands atop Ice Mountain, alone and sorrowful; and, I fear, that she is dying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I took a cutting from ... her ... then I could grow another one.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Yes, but she only has life enough to produce one shoot. Cut this shoot, and she will die, last of her kind gone forever. If you failed to grow this shoot, there would be no more White Trees.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should get out a bit more and quit worrying about trees.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: No, I am afraid I cannot do that. She is the last White Tree, and I am the last to care about her. I will not let her pass away alone and unloved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unless you allow me to do this she will die anyway.

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: I know, but I try not to think about that. You speak truly - she does not have much time left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then will you allow me to take this cutting?

lines.speaker: Dantaera
lines.text: Do so with my blessing. I wish you good fortune, [player name]. Go now, and save the beauty of the White Tree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fish in a well? There can't be anything down there except water!! Perhaps I should try some place where I might expect to find fish - a fishing spot maybe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: la-de-da

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: dum-de-dum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice day for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (whistles a happy tune)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very nice rosebushes you have here.

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: Yes, it has taken me many long hours in this garden to bring them to this state of near-perfection.

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: Are you messing about with my rosebushes again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er ... no ... I was just admiring them.

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: Nonsense, you reek of this evil power! I suggest that you throw whatever dark talisman it is that you are using down the Edgeville well, and never think of it again! Either that or just simply destroy it!

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: Are you messing about with my rosebushes again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er ... no ... I was just admiring them.

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: Ah, I sense that you speak so without the use of the evil power you carried before. That is good. As you have taken the road back towards the light, you may take any seeds that you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have some rosebush seeds, please?

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: No, I think not. These bushes are my pride and joy, and I will not give them away so cheaply.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These are the most beautiful rosebushes I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: Nice of you to say so, but I sense the use of some unnatural power underneath your fine words. You are concealing something of great evil, an evil that cannot touch someone of my purity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh ... no, that's not true ...

lines.speaker: Brother Althric
lines.text: Nonsense, you reek of this evil power! I suggest that you throw whatever dark talisman it is that you are using down the Edgeville well, and never think of it again! Either that or just simply destroy it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (Hmmm ... better not let those guards see me...)

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: So what are you doing this weekend?

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: This weekend?

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: You haven't been here long I take it.

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: Do you know the life expectancy of a Falador guard?

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: About thirty seconds, I reckon.

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: Thirty seconds???

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: They didn't tell me that when I signed up!!!

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: Any second now someone is going to try and kill us, for no apparent reason, completely unprovoked.

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: But why, Bob???

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: What have we ever done to them?

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: I don't know, Billy.

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: But I'm not wasting my final seconds worrying about it.

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: Uh-oh, someone's coming...

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: Bob???

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: Steady, Billy - remember you are a guard of Falador.

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: See you in the next world...

lines.speaker: Bob, another guard of Falador
lines.text: Aaarrrgghhh!!!!

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: NOOOOO!!!!!!!!!

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: Why are you doing this?!?

lines.speaker: Billy, a guard of Falador
lines.text: Aaarrrgghhh!!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm supposed to be getting a statue of a KING from Lumbridge. I'm no expert, but that looks like a QUEEN to me.

sections.title: Talking to Ellamaria with the seeds

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Very good peasant, thank you for showing one that, but one has given you a job to do. Do it.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes, very good peasant, you've got the seed; but it's delphiniums that one wishes, not seeds. Plant it in the delphinium patch, and let it grow.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes, very good peasant, you've got the seed; but it's orchids that one wishes, not seeds. Plant it in the orchid patch, and let it grow.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes, very good peasant, you've got the seed; but it's Burthorpe vine that one wishes, not seeds. Plant it in the Burthorpe vine patch, and let it grow.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes, very good peasant, you've got the seed; but it's a [red/white/yellow] rosebush that one wishes, not seeds. Plant it in the [red/white/yellow] rosebush patch, and let it grow.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes, very good peasant, you've got the seed; but it's snowdrops that one wishes, not seeds. Plant it in the snowdrop patch, and let it grow.

sections.title: Talking to Ellamaria after planting the seeds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think that's everything...

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: One finds that hard to believe. Out of my way, let me see for myself.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: One was right not to trust your word, common as it is. The garden is not yet complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? What haven't I finished yet?

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: I see that the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: Also, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: Thirdly, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh look, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: To add to that, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: And what a surprise, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh you are doing so well, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: To add insult to injury, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: And finally, the [plant name] [hasn't/haven't] finished growing.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: One suggest you learn a little patience. These plants should not take long to grow - these patches have been filled with extremely fertile soil that was very expensive to import.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh yes, very nice, if a little yobbish. Rollie will be so pleased–

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rollie?!?

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: You will address my husband by his proper title!!!!! Speaking of whom, I think it is time that we brought him to see his new garden. And when one says we, one means you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you want me to go and get Roald – sorry ... King Roald ... and bring him here. Fair enough, wait here then, I'll be back in a mo.

sections.title: Talking to Ellamaria before fetching Roald

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: So are you going to get Rollie then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't spoken to Roll ... King Roald ... yet.

lines.speaker: Ellamaria
lines.text: Well hurry along peasant. This garden will be such a wonderful surprise!

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Hey, why are you digging that up? Leave it alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry, didn't mean to...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you like to follow me for a minute?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Follow you?!? I am the King! I am the followed, not the follower!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, if you can't be bothered then neither can I.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Suit yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you suit yourself.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You're quite argumentative for a peasant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not the one who's arguing, you are.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Then why do you keep answering me back?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you're answering me back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, stop talking and I'll stop answering.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: No, you stop talking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have stopped talking.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: No you haven't, you're doing it now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm just answering what you're saying.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Okay, so if I don't say anything, you won't answer me back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Suits me.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Go on, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you first.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: See, that's better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, much better.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: What were you saying about me following you somewhere?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't remember, it was such a long time ago.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Ah, that's a shame - I could have done with a breath of fresh air.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you around.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, see you later.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I thought you were going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was until you spoke to me again.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'll stop speaking to you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you around.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, your majesty - please forgive me.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Forgiveness is something that cannot be earned so easily. And I'm certainly not following you off on some wild goose chase when I have important matters of state to attend to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it - I've had it with royalty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: And I've had enough of peasantry! Get out of my castle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Queen asked me to bring you.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Ellie?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Now that's a different matter. Lead on, peasant - let's see what my darling wife has been up to now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You will have to excuse me then. I am very busy as I have a kingdom to run!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: This is murder on my hay fever you know...

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Ah, Rollie my love!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: What have you been up to now, pumpkin?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Behold your new garden!!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Have you been spending my money again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's some orchids from Morytania.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Orchids bring me out in a rash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Vines from Burthorpe!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Oh great, let's hope they don't eat into the walls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Snowdrops from Ardougne!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: What a boring colour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The last surviving White Tree!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Should make a nice table and chairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Delphiniums from Falador!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I hate delphiniums.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Rollie?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes pumpkin?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: One has gone to great pains to provide you with this beautiful garden!

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: YOU CAN SHOW ONE A LITTLE GRATITUDE!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I think I did most of-

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: SILENCE!!

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Well? Are you going to thank one, Rollie?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Pumpkin I-

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: SPEAK UP, I CAN'T HEAR YOU!!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Thank you very much my darling.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Much better. Now get back to the castle and rule our kingdom.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes pumpkin.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Excuse my husband, he can be so rude sometimes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only sometimes?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Are you being rude again ... ?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Yes, and that's all he deserves! Here you go, your help here has proved invaluable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the supercompost potion for?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Oh, let one see peasant - perhaps it is a potion for making supercompost?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I didn't mean that-

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: You wish to know how to use it? Pour it on some normal compost and it will transform into supercompost. You can do this with a bucket of compost, although it is possibly wiser to use it on a full compost bin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I use this garden for my own farming?

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: What, you want to dig up the plants that took one so long to grow? Don't be ridiculous, you silly little fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All right then, sorry I asked.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Impertinent peasant! Oh, if you promise to leave one alone then feel free to pick fruit from the White Tree. One is told that they are a great natural energy restorative.
